zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel de Malbank於大約1358年出生在英國爾南特維其時，她的父親Piers Malbank39歲，母親Alice de Vernon37歲。她於1382年與Sir Richard de Vernon在英國英格蘭爾可維結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1445年在英國英格蘭爾達文哈姆享年88歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel de Malbank于大约1358年出生在英国爾南特維其时，她的父亲Piers Malbank39岁，母亲Alice de Vernon37岁。她于1382年与Sir Richard de Vernon在英国英格兰爾可維结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1445年在英国英格兰爾達文哈姆享年88岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Malbank
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Malbon  .

variants.name: Malbon
meanings: English (Staffordshire): nickname or habitational name from Norman French Malbanc, which may be from Old French mal banc ‘bad bench’, but if so the reason for the use of this expression as a surname is unknown. Possibly it derives from a lost placename, in which banc may mean ‘steep crag’. The second part of the name, -banc, survives in Maybank , but medieval forms in -ban(e), -bon(e), or -bun are also common and are sometimes later re-interpreted as -born and -bourne, leading to confusion with Melbourne .

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel de Malbank was born about 1358, in Nantwich, Cheshire, England, her father, Piers Malbank, was 39 and her mother, Alice de Vernon, was 37. She married Sir Richard de Vernon in 1382, in Crewe, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in 1445, in Davenham, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 88.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel de Malbank est née environ 1358, à Nantwich, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Piers Malbank, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Alice de Vernon, avait 37 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Richard de Vernon en 1382, à Crewe, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée en 1445, à Davenham, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 88 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel de Malbank um 1358 in Nantwich, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Piers Malbank 39 und ihre Mutter Alice de Vernon 37. Sie hat 1382 in Crewe, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom Sir Richard de Vernon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb 1445 im Alter von 88 Jahren in Davenham, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel de Malbank è nata il circa 1358 nel Nantwich, Cheshire, England, sua padre, Piers Malbank, aveva 39 anni e sua madre, Alice de Vernon, aveva 37 anni. Lei sposato Sir Richard de Vernon nel 1382, nel Crewe, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì nel 1445, nel Davenham, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 88 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel de Malbankが約1358年にイギリスチェシュアイアナントウィチで生まれた時，父Piers Malbankは39歳，母Alice de Vernonは37歳であった。1382年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアクレウでSir Richard de Vernonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1445年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアダベンハムで88歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel de Malbank(이)가 대략 1358년 영국 체실르 난트위치에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Piers Malbank(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice de Vernon(은)는 37세였다. 그녀는 1382년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 크레w에서 Sir Richard de Vernon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1445년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 대븐함에서 88세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel de Malbank nasceu aproximadamente 1358, em Nantwich, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Piers Malbank, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Alice de Vernon, tinha 37 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Richard de Vernon em 1382, em Crewe, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ela faleceu em 1445, em Davenham, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 88 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel de Malbank родилась около 1358 г. в местности нантвич, чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени Piers Malbank было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice de Vernon, 37. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Richard de Vernon в 1382 г. в местности крю, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла в 1445 г. в местности давенам, чешир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 88 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabel de Malbank nació aproximadamente 1358, en Nantwich, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Piers Malbank, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Alice de Vernon, tenía 37 años. Se casó con Sir Richard de Vernon en 1382, en Crewe, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió en 1445, en Davenham, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 88 años.
